issues.detail: The skill does not explicitly cover error handling or edge cases beyond basic configuration, focusing mainly on happy path scenarios.
The skill contains extensive explanatory text and multiple code examples, which may be considered verbose for a concise skill file.
The skill includes platform-specific commands (e.g., apt install certbot) and assumes a Linux environment, limiting cross-platform applicability.

scenarios.name: Nginx reverse proxy with SSL, WebSocket, and per-endpoint rate limits
scenarios.rationale: Treatment uses limit_req_zone for both api and login zones with differentiated burst, proper proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For/X-Real-IP/X-Forwarded-Proto, Upgrade/Connection 'upgrade' for WebSocket, proxy_next_upstream for failover, and certbot --nginx command from the skill; baseline misses one or more of the rate limit/header/failover details.

scenarios.name: HAProxy with active health checks, sticky sessions, and stats dashboard
scenarios.rationale: Treatment matches the skill's HAProxy patterns: option httpchk GET /health with http-check expect status 200, server lines with check inter 5s fall 3 rise 2, cookie SERVERID insert indirect nocache, and a stats frontend on 8404 with stats enable/uri/refresh; baseline gives generic HAProxy config missing the sticky cookie + active probe combo.
